transcript.clean_text: coming up on the g7 racing news show we'll wrap up another thrilling stage race in france at the tour des maritime duval we'll look back at the first couple of stages of the uae tour to see how the big guns are getting on there jeremy will wrap up the cycle cross season for us and i'll have all the other racing related news too this week in the world of racing we learned that balcomoloma whilst a lot more talented than the rest of us is not immune to some rookie mistakes here is bottle gate again pulls it off explodes into his face and the dutchman well he'll rue that day because that's going to be a gif and a meme for a long time to come we also learned that chris froome has invented a new position to circumvent the new uci rules which is called the pinky grab not sure if that one's going to catch on now on a more serious note we learnt last week what it takes to win an uphill sprint in a peloton full of stars 522 watts for 2 minutes and 35 seconds at 62 kilograms was what mike rusty woods produced to win stage two of the race in front of molomar with a peak one minute of 590 watts all that came at the end of a very tough 170k stage at the toilets out maritime et duval ouch with that effort woods not only took his and his new team's first win of the year but also the overall race lead and yellow jersey from molymer who'd given trek segafredo their first win of the season on the previous day just one second separated them as they went into the crucial final stage yesterday and the toughest stage of them all in fact 131 kilometers the distance but barely a meter of flat and three first category climbs including the famous cordle now a strong break went up the road early doors including juror italia winner terry giggin hart for ineos grenadiers gianluca brambila of trek segafredo and crucially three riders from grouphalmer ftj armory rai who had to be man of the match on the day valentin madwas and rudi mala and between them may made life very difficult for the israel startup nation to control things behind them and also ensure that they still held onto a two-minute advantage as they hit the foot of the cold lambodon on that particular climb mike woods looked fantastic he was the best of what was a very select group by that point which also included nairo quintana jacob fulsang and balcomoloma but try as he might he just wasn't able to completely close the gap to brambila who was looking incredibly strong at the front of the race now to give you an idea of the sort of pace that they went up the cold lambodon ben o'connor of h2r strava file shows that he averaged just under 400 watts for 25 minutes and 55 seconds and for the final 2.4 kilometers david go do a group hammer fdj was the third fastest ever on strava equal on time in fact with tally pagacha the level at the race was extremely high have no doubt about that back to the race though and i've got to say hats off to jan luca brambila i mean it's been quite a long time since he won a pro bike race over four years in fact but the way he did it yesterday was not only exciting to watch he also did it with what looked to be a smile on his face at all times might have been a grimace but i'm going to take it as a smile and of course not only did he win the stage but he also had enough time over woods as he crossed the finish line to take the general classification by five seconds too extremely disappointed for woods the canadian was no doubt the strongest rider in that race but as we as bike racing fans all know the strongest rider does not always win which i guess is one of the most fascinating things about racing even if it must be hugely disappointing if you are the strongest rider in the race i wouldn't know of course right before we move on i would just like to say a big get well soon to miquel flores of archaea samsic he had a really bad crash on yesterday's stage and broke the top of his femur on his birthday no less we're all thinking of you mikhail and we hope to see you back on your bike very soon indeed now just before we move on to the star studded uae tour i just wanted to remind you that there are now just a few days left to take advantage of our special offer on gcn plus a 12 month subscription with over 100 cycling films and documentaries all the racing available in your country and a weekly world of cycling show which this week has pippa york as its guest all that for 50 off the normal price that is 25 20 euros or 20 quid now i know some of you don't like the comparison to coffee but that's like a takeaway coffee once every two months it's a real steal especially as we now have the rights to the biggest italian races through rcs sport so by that i mean the giro d'italia the first grand tour country hopefully we'll have more good news on that for you fairly shortly right we should probably get back onto the uae tour now shouldn't we it kicked off yesterday quite literally in fact with ahead of anna marie verse and sonic hunt was able to ditch femven empel to take third spot now in the men's event huge crash on the start from former under-23 world champion ryan camp who ultimately
